name: SLEW
description: Voltage-controlled lag processor with independent rise and fall faders · three-position speed range · linear-to-time-compensated SHAPE · rising and falling gate outputs from onboard slope detector · <1mV pitch tracking error.

controls.name: RISE
controls.description: Fader sets rise slew time. Also attenuates CV IN when the CV selector routes to rise.
controls.detail: bottom: instant · top: max range · doubles as CV attenuator

controls.name: FALL
controls.description: Fader sets fall slew time. Also attenuates CV IN when the CV selector routes to fall.
controls.detail: bottom: instant · top: max range · doubles as CV attenuator

controls.name: SHAPE
controls.description: Morphs curve from linear to time-compensated. CW increases the rate of change as the gap between IN and OUT grows.
controls.detail: CCW: linear (constant dV/dt) · CW: distance-driven · time-compensated portamento at full CW

controls.name: RANGE
controls.description: Three-position switch selecting the overall slew time scale.

controls.name: R / RF / F
controls.description: Three-position switch selecting whether CV IN modulates RISE only, both, or FALL only.
controls.detail: up (R): rise · center (RF): both · down (F): fall

inputs.name: IN
inputs.description: Signal to be slewed. Tracks pitch CV with <1mV error so portamento stays in tune.

inputs.name: CV
inputs.description: CV over slew time. Destination set by the R/RF/F switch; attenuated by the matching fader.

outputs.name: OUT
outputs.description: Slew-limited copy of IN. Converts a gate into an ASR-style envelope when RISE and FALL are set to taste.

outputs.name: RISING
outputs.description: Gate high while OUT is rising. Slope detector sits after the slew so sensitivity scales with the current slew time.

outputs.name: FALLING
outputs.description: Gate high while OUT is falling. Same detector as RISING, mirrored polarity.

behaviors.name: Time-compensated portamento
behaviors.trigger: SHAPE turned fully clockwise with pitch CV on IN
behaviors.description: Rate of change scales with note-to-note distance so wide intervals glide in roughly the same time as small ones. Keeps acid-line slides musical across the keyboard instead of dragging on large leaps.

behaviors.name: Gate-to-ASR envelope
behaviors.trigger: gate patched to IN with RISE and FALL set above zero
behaviors.description: Rising edge climbs at the rise rate, gate-high holds at the gate voltage (sustain), falling edge decays at the fall rate. One fader per segment gives attack and release without a dedicated envelope module.

behaviors.name: Slope-driven event gates
behaviors.description: RISING outputs a gate while OUT climbs, FALLING while it drops. Use to trigger envelopes or advance a counter whenever a melody moves up or down. Sensitivity rises with the RANGE switch toward fastest — tighten RISE/FALL to kill ghost gates from ripple.

behaviors.name: Faders as CV attenuators
behaviors.description: RISE and FALL faders stop being absolute times and become attenuators scaling CV IN into that parameter. Lower the fader to reduce modulation depth without needing an external attenuator.

behaviors.name: Ghost-gate taming
behaviors.trigger: RANGE on fastest with quiet source
behaviors.description: At minimum slew the detector catches sub-mV ripple and outputs unwanted gates. Raise slew time or the RANGE switch until the gates lock to intentional transitions only.

behaviors.name: Linear vs distance-driven response
behaviors.description: CCW keeps dV/dt constant — classic slew. As SHAPE opens, larger input jumps become proportionally faster, preventing long glides from feeling flat. Mid-position sits between the two for hybrid behaviour.

patchIdeas.name: Classic acid slide
patchIdeas.patch: Sequencer pitch → IN · RISE low · FALL low · SHAPE full CW for time-compensated glide · OUT → VCO V/Oct · short glides between steps regardless of interval size · sub-mV tracking keeps tuning locked
patchIdeas.diagram:
SLEW:
* RISE: low
* FALL: low
* SHAPE: full CW
* RANGE: middle

Sequencer:
VCO:

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> SLEW (IN)
- SLEW (OUT) p> VCO (V/Oct)

patchIdeas.name: Gate into ASR envelope
patchIdeas.patch: Gate → IN · RISE = attack · FALL = release · OUT → VCA CV · sustain = gate voltage · free AR/ASR envelope from any gate source · use RANGE middle for musical timings
patchIdeas.diagram:
SLEW:
* RISE: attack time
* FALL: release time
* SHAPE: CCW (linear)
* RANGE: middle

Clock:
VCA:
VCO:

- Clock (Out) g> SLEW (IN)
- VCO (Out) -> VCA (In)
- SLEW (OUT) >> VCA (CV)

patchIdeas.name: Up/down event detector
patchIdeas.patch: LFO → IN · RISING and FALLING gates drive separate envelopes · each half of the LFO fires a different event · use on slow random CV to generate rhythmic triggers tied to voltage direction
patchIdeas.diagram:
SLEW:
* RISE: low
* FALL: low
* RANGE: fastest

LFO:
Envelope A:
Envelope B:

- LFO (Out) >> SLEW (IN)
- SLEW (RISING) t> Envelope A (Trig)
- SLEW (FALLING) t> Envelope B (Trig)

patchIdeas.name: Voltage-controlled glide depth
patchIdeas.patch: Sequencer pitch → IN · OUT → VCO · expression pedal or LFO into CV · switch to RF to modulate both rise and fall · RISE and FALL faders set max glide time · dynamic portamento that opens and closes under CV
patchIdeas.diagram:
SLEW:
* RISE: mid
* FALL: mid
* CV switch: RF
* SHAPE: 2 o'clock

Sequencer:
LFO:
VCO:

- Sequencer (Pitch) p> SLEW (IN)
- LFO (Out) >> SLEW (CV)
- SLEW (OUT) p> VCO (V/Oct)

patchIdeas.name: Asymmetric kick envelope
patchIdeas.patch: Trigger → IN · RISE almost zero for punchy attack · FALL moderate for body · SHAPE CCW for linear decay · OUT → VCA and filter cutoff · no dedicated envelope needed for simple percussive shapes
patchIdeas.diagram:
SLEW:
* RISE: 0 (instant)
* FALL: mid
* SHAPE: CCW
* RANGE: fastest

Clock:
VCO:
Filter:
VCA:

- Clock (Out) t> SLEW (IN)
- VCO (Out) -> Filter (In)
- Filter (Out) -> VCA (In)
- SLEW (OUT) >> VCA (CV)
- SLEW (OUT) >> Filter (Cutoff)

patchIdeas.name: Smooth sample-and-hold
patchIdeas.patch: S&H output → IN · RANGE middle, RISE and FALL matched · OUT → filter cutoff · stepped random becomes smooth random contour · SHAPE 2 o'clock so big jumps still arrive on time
patchIdeas.diagram:
SLEW:
* RISE: mid
* FALL: mid
* SHAPE: 2 o'clock
* RANGE: middle

S&H:
Filter:

- S&H (Out) >> SLEW (IN)
- SLEW (OUT) >> Filter (Cutoff)
